title: salix × koidzumii
intro: Salix × koidzumii, also known as Koidzum's willow or Japanese white willow, is a hybrid willow species derived from the crossing of Salix alba and Salix matsudana. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, derived from the salicylic acid content in its bark. The health benefits of Salix × koidzumii include relief from pain, reduced fever, and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a potential treatment for various conditions such as arthritis and headaches. In herbal preparations, the bark of the Salix × koidzumii is typically dried and processed into teas, tinctures, or capsules for medicinal use.
